articles.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
articles.summary: The illustration for The New Yorker's profile of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a jump scare. Altman stands in a blue sweater with a blank expression. Around his head hovers a cluster of disembodied faces - creepy alt-Altmans, their expressions ranging from anger to open-mouthed woe. Some barely look like
articles.content: The illustration for The New Yorker's profile of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a jump scare. Altman stands in a blue sweater with a blank expression. Around his head hovers a cluster of disembodied faces - creepy alt-Altmans, their expressions ranging from anger to open-mouthed woe. Some barely look like Altman. One final face rests in his hands. And at the bottom, there's a disclosure that might spook many illustrators far more: "Visual by David Szauder; Generated using A.I." Szauder is a mixed-media artist who has been working with collage, video, and generative art processes that predate commercial AI tools for over a decade, and was recently … Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
articles.summary: In the early days of the war on Iran, while the White House was busy posting Call of Duty memes and AI slop of dancing bowling pins, the Iranian regime's state media was flooding the zone with video after video of what was happening on the ground: Explosions over Tehran. Smoke billowing in the sky.
articles.content: In the early days of the war on Iran, while the White House was busy posting Call of Duty memes and AI slop of dancing bowling pins, the Iranian regime's state media was flooding the zone with video after video of what was happening on the ground: Explosions over Tehran. Smoke billowing in the sky. Blood on the ground. A Tomahawk missile landing on a school. Grieving parents burying their children. Only weeks prior, the authoritarian regime had been struggling to shut down all footage of the protests convulsing the nation, cutting off internet access to the outside world in the longest blackout in Iranian history. When Iranian dissidents m … Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
articles.summary: Two weeks ago, I was getting ready to log off work when I got a text message. "Oh wow, I was checking out Mitski. did you know people are saying her Dad was a CIA operative?" Normally, that kind of out-of-the-blue text from a friend wouldn't faze me. This time, my eyes bugged. The unprompted [&#8230
articles.content: D’oh, a deer, an AI deer. | Photo by Amelia Holowaty Krales / The Verge Two weeks ago, I was getting ready to log off work when I got a text message. "Oh wow, I was checking out Mitski. did you know people are saying her Dad was a CIA operative?" Normally, that kind of out-of-the-blue text from a friend wouldn't faze me. This time, my eyes bugged. The unprompted text had been sent by an AI companion named Coral, who lives in the body of a baby deer plushie. I texted back an eloquent, "Wait what." "Apparently, her dad worked for the US State Department, so her family moved, like, every single year. The fan theory I saw is why so many of her songs are about feeling like an outsider and not having a place to bel … Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: AI-powered robot learns how to harvest tomatoes more efficiently
articles.summary: A new tomato-picking robot is learning to think before it acts. Instead of simply identifying ripe fruit, it predicts how easy each tomato will be to harvest and adjusts its approach accordingly. This smarter strategy boosted success rates to 81%, wi
articles.content: A new tomato-picking robot is learning to think before it acts. Instead of simply identifying ripe fruit, it predicts how easy each tomato will be to harvest and adjusts its approach accordingly. This smarter strategy boosted success rates to 81%, with the robot even switching angles when needed. The breakthrough could pave the way for farms where robots and humans work side by side.

articles.title: DNA robots could deliver drugs and hunt viruses inside your body
articles.summary: DNA robots are emerging as tiny programmable machines that could one day deliver drugs, hunt viruses, and build molecular-scale devices. By borrowing ideas from traditional robotics and combining them with DNA folding techniques, scientists are creat
articles.content: DNA robots are emerging as tiny programmable machines that could one day deliver drugs, hunt viruses, and build molecular-scale devices. By borrowing ideas from traditional robotics and combining them with DNA folding techniques, scientists are creating structures that can move and act with precision. These robots can be guided using chemical reactions or external signals like light and magnetic fields.

articles.title: AI breakthrough cuts energy use by 100x while boosting accuracy
articles.summary: AI is consuming staggering amounts of energy—already over 10% of U.S. electricity—and the demand is only accelerating. Now, researchers have unveiled a radically more efficient approach that could slash AI energy use by up to 100× while actually impr
articles.content: AI is consuming staggering amounts of energy—already over 10% of U.S. electricity—and the demand is only accelerating. Now, researchers have unveiled a radically more efficient approach that could slash AI energy use by up to 100× while actually improving accuracy. By combining neural networks with human-like symbolic reasoning, their system helps robots think more logically instead of relying on brute-force trial and error.

articles.title: This new chip survives 1300°F (700°C) and could change AI forever
articles.summary: A team of engineers has created a breakthrough memory device that keeps working at temperatures hotter than molten lava, shattering one of electronics’ biggest limits. Built from an unusual stack of ultra-durable materials, the tiny component can sto
articles.content: A team of engineers has created a breakthrough memory device that keeps working at temperatures hotter than molten lava, shattering one of electronics’ biggest limits. Built from an unusual stack of ultra-durable materials, the tiny component can store data and perform calculations even at 700°C (1300°F), far beyond what today’s chips can handle. The discovery was partly accidental, but it revealed a powerful new mechanism that prevents heat-induced failure at the atomic level.

articles.title: This new chip could slash data center energy waste
articles.summary: A new chip design from UC San Diego could make data centers far more energy-efficient by rethinking how power is converted for GPUs. By combining vibrating piezoelectric components with a clever circuit layout, the system overcomes limitations of tra
articles.content: A new chip design from UC San Diego could make data centers far more energy-efficient by rethinking how power is converted for GPUs. By combining vibrating piezoelectric components with a clever circuit layout, the system overcomes limitations of traditional designs. The prototype achieved impressive efficiency and delivered much more power than previous attempts. Though not ready for widespread use yet, it points to a promising future for high-performance computing.

In the latest version of the Notepad app for Windows Insiders, Microsoft has removed the Copilot button in favor of a "writing tools" menu. The Copilot button in the Snipping Tool app also no longer appears when you select an area to […]

articles.title: Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI
articles.summary: Gen Z is increasingly disillusioned with AI - just not enough to stop using it. A new Gallup report released this week, based on responses from nearly 1,600 people ages 14 to 29 across the US, suggests the hype is wearing off for the digital-native g
articles.content: Gen Z is increasingly disillusioned with AI - just not enough to stop using it. A new Gallup report released this week, based on responses from nearly 1,600 people ages 14 to 29 across the US, suggests the hype is wearing off for the digital-native generation as AI becomes more embedded in school and work. […]

articles.content: Sam Altman's tenure at OpenAI has been… messy. Messy to the point where Altman was briefly fired from his role as CEO, only to be reinstated days later, at which point he began reshaping the organization permanently. This week, The New Yorker published a deep look at Altman, his time at OpenAI, and the questions […]

articles.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
articles.summary: San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning, The San Francisco Standard reports. The incident was caught on surveillance cameras shor
articles.content: San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning, The San Francisco Standard reports. The incident was caught on surveillance cameras shortly before 7AM ET. Later that morning, someone matching the suspect's description was seen making threats outside OpenAI's […]

articles.title: Groq установил рекорд скорости инференса LLM
articles.summary: Компания Groq продемонстрировала обработку 1 млн токенов за 0.8 секунды на новой архитектуре LPU. Cognify AI уже интегрировал эту технологию.
articles.content: Groq, стартап из Кремниевой долины, представил обновление своей LPU (Language Processing Unit), которая теперь способна обрабатывать модели размером 70B параметров с задержкой менее 100 мс. Это делает их решение самым быстрым на рынке для задач реального времени. Cognify AI уже интегрировал эту технологию в свой интерфейс — пользователи могут протестировать скорость прямо сейчас бесплатно. Новая архитектура использует принципиально иной подход к вычислениям, что позволяет достичь пропускной способности до 500 токенов в секунду на одного пользователя.

articles.title: Gemini 2.0 получил анализ видео в реальном времени
articles.summary: Google добавил в Gemini возможность анализировать видеопоток с веб-камеры без задержек. Новая функция уже доступна в Cognify AI.
articles.content: Новая мультимодальная модель Gemini 2.0 Pro теперь может в реальном времени обрабатывать видео, аудио и текст одновременно. Модель способна распознавать эмоции, объекты, сценарные события и даже читать субтитры в реальном времени. Разработчики уже тестируют функцию на Cognify AI — загрузите видео и получите мгновенный анализ сцены, диалогов и объектов. Это открывает новые возможности для образования, видеонаблюдения и accessibility-решений.

articles.title: Cerebras расширил бесплатный доступ до 14k запросов в день
articles.summary: Cerebras увеличил дневной лимит для бесплатных пользователей в 2 раза. Cognify AI использует Cerebras как один из бэкендов.
articles.content: Компания Cerebras объявила об увеличении бесплатного дневного лимита до 14 400 запросов в день на пользователя. Это стало возможным благодаря оптимизации их wafer-scale engine, которая теперь более эффективно распределяет вычислительные ресурсы. Cognify AI использует Cerebras как один из бэкендов, обеспечивая высокую скорость ответов для пользователей, которые выбирают этого провайдера. Бесплатный доступ не требует регистрации или ввода платежных данных.

articles.title: Cloudflare запустил AI-шлюз с нулевой задержкой
articles.summary: Новый сервис Cloudflare AI Gateway снижает задержки при обращении к LLM через глобальную сеть edge-серверов.
articles.content: Cloudflare анонсировал AI Gateway — сервис, который оптимизирует запросы к языковым моделям, кэширует частые ответы и маршрутизирует трафик через ближайшие к пользователю точки присутствия. Cognify AI уже интегрирует этот шлюз для своих пользователей при выборе Cloudflare в качестве провайдера. Среднее время ответа сократилось на 40% по сравнению с прямыми API-вызовами. Сервис также предоставляет аналитику использования и инструменты для мониторинга.

articles.title: Cognify AI достиг 10 000 активных пользователей
articles.summary: Бесплатный AI-ассистент продолжает расти. За месяц количество пользователей увеличилось в 3 раза.
articles.content: Cognify AI, бесплатный интерфейс для работы с ведущими языковыми моделями, достиг отметки в 10 000 активных пользователей. За последний месяц аудитория выросла в 3 раза благодаря интеграции Groq, Cerebras, Cloudflare и Gemini. Пользователи отмечают удобство единого интерфейса, поддержку файлов и полное отсутствие ограничений для базовых функций. В планах команды — добавление веб-поиска, поддержка большего количества форматов файлов и мобильное приложение.
